text: ‘Whoever thou art’ probably implies that verses 1-16 are addressed both to Jews and to Gentiles.

The meaning of the last clause in this verse is disputed; but it seems likely that St Paul is alluding to the Stoic philosophers, who classed some things as ‘preferred’ and others as ‘rejected’; a third class they called ‘things of no moment’, and there may be an allusion to this in verse 18 below.
